description_long: This kiosk is a modern, open-structure installation with multiple partitioned panels arranged on a flat, polygonal base. The panels are primarily white, with some featuring large printed graphics and images, likely for informational or advertising purposes. The structure is open on all sides, allowing easy access and visibility, and does not appear to have a roof or enclosed walls. The panels vary in height and width, creating a dynamic, segmented appearance, and some panels display what looks like architectural or real estate imagery. The overall design is minimalist, with clean lines and a focus on visual communication through signage. The kiosk is likely intended for use in public spaces such as plazas, parks, or exhibition areas.
description: A modern, open-sided kiosk with white partition panels displaying large printed graphics, arranged on a flat polygonal base. The structure is minimalist and designed for easy public access and visual communication.
description_view_0: From this view, the kiosk's largest graphic panel is prominent, with several other panels forming a semi-enclosed space on a flat base.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the arrangement of the panels, showing more of the interior graphics and the open, accessible layout.
description_view_2: Here, the kiosk's segmented structure and the variety of printed graphics on the panels are visible, emphasizing the open design.
description_view_3: This view shows the rear and side panels, with more printed graphics and the open, walk-through nature of the kiosk.
attribute_signage_type: printed sign and graphics on side and central panels, featuring images and text integrated into the structure
attribute_roof_design: none; open-air with no visible roof structure
attribute_architectural_style: modern minimalist with open, modular panel arrangement
